## Item Mapping

### G1 Angle Reasoning (8 items)
| ID | Nano‑Node | Equation Shadow | Difficulty Zone | Notes |
|----|-----------|----------------|----------------|-------|
| G001 | Identify and measure angles using a protractor | `angle = protractor_reading` | A | Measure acute, obtuse, right angles |
| G002 | Use angle properties to find unknown angles (angles on straight line) | `∠A + ∠B = 180°` | B | Linear pair, one angle given |
| G003 | Use angle properties to find unknown angles (angles at a point) | `∠A + ∠B + ∠C = 360°` | B | Angles around a point |
| G004 | Use angle properties to find unknown angles (vertically opposite angles) | `∠A = ∠B` | B | Intersecting lines |
| G005 | Apply angle sum of triangle (180°) to find missing angles | `∠A + ∠B + ∠C = 180°` | C | Triangle with two angles given |
| G006 | Identify perpendicular and parallel lines in diagrams | N/A (visual identification) | A | Mark perpendicular/parallel lines in a figure |
| G007 | Combined angle properties (multi‑step) | `∠A + ∠B = 180°` and `∠B + ∠C = 90°` | D | Two‑step reasoning |
| G008 | Measure reflex angles (protractor) | `angle = protractor_reading` (180‑360°) | C | Reflex angle measurement |

### G2 Area & Perimeter (8 items)
| ID | Nano‑Node | Equation Shadow | Difficulty Zone | Notes |
|----|-----------|----------------|----------------|-------|
| G009 | Calculate perimeter of rectilinear figures | `P = Σ side_lengths` | A | Simple rectangle perimeter |
| G010 | Calculate perimeter of rectilinear figures with missing lengths | `P = Σ side_lengths` (infer missing) | B | Composite shape, find missing side |
| G011 | Calculate area of composite rectangular figures | `total_area = Σ(area_of_rectangle_i)` | B | L‑shape composite figure |
| G012 | Calculate area of composite rectangular figures with missing sections | `total_area = Σ(area_of_rectangle_i)` | C | Figure with cut‑out rectangle |
| G013 | Convert between units of measurement (length) | `1 m = 100 cm` | A | Convert cm to m and vice versa |
| G014 | Convert between units of measurement (area) | `1 m² = 10 000 cm²` | B | Conversion involving squared units |
| G015 | Identify line(s) of symmetry in 2D shapes | N/A (visual) | A | Draw symmetry lines in regular polygon |
| G016 | Complete symmetrical figures given mirror line | `(x, y) → (x, −y)` (vertical reflection) | C | Reflect shape across vertical/horizontal line |

### G3 Volume & 3D (4 items)
| ID | Nano‑Node | Equation Shadow | Difficulty Zone | Notes |
|----|-----------|----------------|----------------|-------|
| G017 | Calculate volume of cubes and cuboids | `V = l × b × h` | A | Simple cuboid volume |
| G018 | Calculate volume of cubes and cuboids with unit conversion | `V = l × b × h` (convert units) | B | Mixed units (cm and m) |
| G019 | Visualize nets of cubes/cuboids and identify 3D shapes from nets | N/A (spatial reasoning) | C | Match net to 3D shape |
| G020 | Visualize nets of cubes/cuboids (draw net given 3D shape) | N/A (drawing) | D | Draw net of given cuboid |

### G4 Properties & Classification (5 items)
| ID | Nano‑Node | Equation Shadow | Difficulty Zone | Notes |
|----|-----------|----------------|----------------|-------|
| G021 | Classify triangles by sides (equilateral, isosceles, scalene) | N/A (classification) | A | Identify triangle type from side lengths |
| G022 | Classify triangles by angles (acute, right, obtuse) | N/A (classification) | A | Identify triangle type from angle measures |
| G023 | Classify quadrilaterals (square, rectangle, parallelogram, rhombus, trapezium) | N/A (classification) | B | Identify quadrilateral type from properties |
| G024 | Calculate circumference/area of circle using π | `C = π × d` or `A = π × r²` | C | Given radius or diameter |
| G025 | Interpret pie charts as fractions of a circle | `fraction = angle / 360°` | D | Calculate proportion from pie chart sector |

## Difficulty Zones (A–D)
- **A:** Basic recall, single‑step, low cognitive load (foundation)
- **B:** Standard application, one or two steps, familiar context
- **C:** Multi‑step reasoning, unfamiliar context, unit conversion
- **D:** Complex problem‑solving, integration of multiple concepts, spatial reasoning
